enforcement.description: Honesty signal for effect providers. Providers declaring contain/block capability SHOULD set enforcement:real ONLY when they actually apply OS-level containment. Stubs, plumbing, and capability-only providers declare enforcement:simulated. Absent is conceptually equivalent to "simulated".

enforced_actions.description: Action-scoped capability (item 8): the action types this provider's real enforcement actually covers (e.g. ["network_connect","network_fetch","dns_lookup"] for a network-only sandbox). Optional and ONLY meaningful with enforcement:real. Empty or absent means the provider enforces every action it is asked about (the backward-compatible default, e.g. a full-containment devcontainer). When set, the kernel classifies enforceability=enforces only for a covered action and degrades to detects for any other — a partial enforcer cannot over-claim. Forward-looking: declared by providers; no shipping provider partially-enforces yet.
